title: Expression language comparison — custom minimal evaluator vs CEL adoption
question: Should the workflow expression language remain the current bespoke 339-line property-access evaluator, adopt Google's Common Expression Language (CEL) wholesale, or adopt a CEL hybrid — and what are the tradeoffs?
scope: systeminit/swamp main branch cloned 2026-04-15 (CEL adoption reference)
findings_summary:
Our expression language is a deliberate, minimal, security-audited, 339-line bespoke evaluator supporting ${{ path.to.value | filter }} syntax with nine built-in filters (duration, currency, json, length, keys, filter, first, last, slugify, shell). It deliberately excludes arithmetic, comparisons, logical operators, ternary, string concatenation, and function calls — pushing logic into transform steps per documented design rationale.

CEL (Common Expression Language) is Google's non-Turing-complete, bounded-evaluation, sandboxed expression engine used in Kubernetes validation rules, gRPC, Firebase Security Rules, and Envoy. The swamp project (systeminit/swamp) adopts cel-js with the same ${{ }} delimiter convention. CEL supports arithmetic, comparisons, logical operators, ternary, typed string/list/map operations, and caller-registered functions with deterministic evaluation guarantees.

Cost of staying: every workflow needing boolean logic or arithmetic requires a transform step; contributors and LLMs must learn project-specific grammar (CEL is in training distributions via K8s/Firebase docs); each extension (==, !=, >, <) is custom code.

Benefits of staying: smaller attack surface (339 lines, zero supply-chain risk); security-audited shell filter for command-step single-quote context; explicit ExpressionError diagnostics scoped to our domain.

Cleanest adoption path: keep ${{ }} delimiters, replace property-access-split implementation with CEL evaluator, retain scope shape (input, steps, runId, etc.), register nine filters as CEL functions, retire transform step's reason-for-being. Adjacent ${{ }}${{ }} parse limitation (workflow-extension.md:253) would resolve. Most existing expressions are valid CEL (field-selection operations). Risk: CEL has different null-propagation semantics; shell filter security must be re-audited.

No decision entry exists — no forcing issue currently requires resolving the CEL question. Research stands alone until a forcing function emerges.

citations.label: No eval, deliberate constraints, excellent diagnostics
